1→// Package model defines shared domain types used across the control plane.
     2→//
     3→// These types are the canonical Go representations of the structures defined
     4→// in the OpenAPI specification. All SPI packages import from here rather than
     5→// defining their own copies.
     6→package model
     7→
     8→import "time"
     9→
    10→// ActorRef identifies who or what performed an action.
    11→type ActorRef struct {
    12→	ID          string
    13→	Type        ActorType
    14→	DisplayName string
    15→}
    16→
    17→// ActorType classifies the actor.
    18→type ActorType string
    19→
    20→const (
    21→	ActorTypeAgent            ActorType = "agent"
    22→	ActorTypeHuman            ActorType = "human"
    23→	ActorTypeService          ActorType = "service"
    24→	ActorTypeConnectorRuntime ActorType = "connector_runtime"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// ActionTarget describes the resource an action operates on.
    28→type ActionTarget struct {
    29→	Kind        string
    30→	ID          string
    31→	DisplayName string
    32→}
    33→
    34→// ActionIntent describes what an agent intends to do.
    35→type ActionIntent struct {
    36→	// Type is a dot-namespaced action identifier, e.g. "payment.charge",
    37→	// "git.pull_request.create", "calendar.event.create".
    38→	Type          string
    39→	Summary       string
    40→	Justification string
    41→	Target        ActionTarget
    42→	Domain        DomainAction
    43→	Metadata      map[string]any
    44→}
    45→
    46→// DomainAction carries action-type-specific fields.
    47→// Exactly one field should be populated, corresponding to the ActionIntent.Type prefix.
    48→type DomainAction struct {
    49→	Git         *GitAction
    50→	Deploy      *DeployAction
    51→	Cloud       *CloudAction
    52→	Email       *EmailAction
    53→	Calendar    *CalendarAction
    54→	Payment     *PaymentAction
    55→	Procurement *ProcurementAction
    56→}
    57→
    58→// Money represents an amount in minor units (e.g. cents) with a currency code.
    59→type Money struct {
    60→	Amount   int64
    61→	Currency string // ISO 4217, e.g. "USD"
    62→}
    63→
    64→// GitAction carries git/source-control-specific intent fields.
    65→type GitAction struct {
    66→	Provider       string // "github", "gitlab", "bitbucket", "custom"
    67→	Repository     string
    68→	Branch         string
    69→	BaseBranch     string
    70→	CommitSHAs     []string
    71→	FilesChanged   []string
    72→	PRTitle        string
    73→	PRBody         string
    74→	Labels         []string
    75→	Reviewers      []string
    76→	ChecksRequired []string
    77→}
    78→
    79→// DeployAction carries deployment-specific intent fields.
    80→type DeployAction struct {
    81→	Provider            string // "kubernetes", "vercel", "netlify", etc.
    82→	Service             string
    83→	Environment         string
    84→	ArtifactRef         string
    85→	ImageRef            string
    86→	Cluster             string
    87→	Namespace           string
    88→	Strategy            string // "rolling", "blue_green", "canary", "replace", "custom"
    89→	RollbackSupported   bool
    90→	ChangeTicketID      string
    91→	MaintenanceWindowID string
    92→}
    93→
    94→// CloudAction carries cloud-resource-specific intent fields.
    95→type CloudAction struct {
    96→	Provider      string // "aws", "gcp", "azure", "cloudflare", "custom"
    97→	AccountID     string
    98→	Region        string
    99→	ResourceType  string
   100→	ResourceID    string
   101→	Operation     string
   102→	EstimatedCost *Money
   103→	Tags          map[string]string
   104→}
   105→
   106→// EmailAction carries email-specific intent fields.
   107→type EmailAction struct {
   108→	From        *Recipient
   109→	To          []Recipient
   110→	CC          []Recipient
   111→	BCC         []Recipient
   112→	Subject     string
   113→	BodyText    string
   114→	BodyHTML    string
   115→	Attachments []AttachmentRef
   116→	ThreadRef   string
   117→	SendMode    string // "send_now", "draft_only"
   118→}
   119→
   120→// Recipient identifies an email participant.
   121→type Recipient struct {
   122→	Name  string
   123→	Email string
   124→}
   125→
   126→// AttachmentRef references a file attachment.
   127→type AttachmentRef struct {
   128→	Name       string
   129→	MIMEType   string
   130→	URL        string
   131→	StorageRef string
   132→}
   133→
   134→// CalendarAction carries calendar-specific intent fields.
   135→type CalendarAction struct {
   136→	Provider       string // "google_calendar", "outlook", "custom"
   137→	Title          string
   138→	Description    string
   139→	Attendees      []CalendarAttendee
   140→	StartTime      *time.Time
   141→	EndTime        *time.Time
   142→	Timezone       string
   143→	Location       string
   144→	ConferenceType string // "none", "google_meet", "zoom", "teams", "custom"
   145→	CalendarID     string
   146→	Visibility     string // "default", "public", "private"
   147→}
   148→
   149→// CalendarAttendee is a participant in a calendar event.
   150→type CalendarAttendee struct {
   151→	Name     string
   152→	Email    string
   153→	Optional bool
   154→}
   155→
   156→// PaymentAction carries payment-specific intent fields.
   157→type PaymentAction struct {
   158→	VendorName                   string
   159→	VendorID                     string
   160→	Amount                       Money
   161→	MerchantCategory             string
   162→	BudgetCode                   string
   163→	FundingSourceID              string
   164→	PaymentInstrumentPreference  string // "virtual_card", "ach", "wallet", "network_proxy", "unspecified"
   165→	Beneficiary                  *PaymentBeneficiary
   166→	LineItems                    []LineItem
   167→	Renewal                      bool
   168→	ContractTerm                 string
   169→	RecurringInterval            string // "none", "monthly", "quarterly", "annual"
   170→	MerchantReference            string
   171→}
   172→
   173→// PaymentBeneficiary identifies the person or team benefiting from a payment.
   174→type PaymentBeneficiary struct {
   175→	Name       string
   176→	Email      string
   177→	Department string
   178→}
   179→
   180→// LineItem is a single item in a payment or procurement request.
   181→type LineItem struct {
   182→	Description string
   183→	Quantity    int
   184→	UnitAmount  Money
   185→	SKU         string
   186→}
   187→
   188→// ProcurementAction carries procurement-specific intent fields.
   189→type ProcurementAction struct {
   190→	RequestType            string // "software", "travel", "equipment", "contractor", "services", "other"
   191→	VendorName             string
   192→	AmountEstimate         *Money
   193→	Justification          string
   194→	Requestor              string
   195→	CostCenter             string
   196→	LegalReviewRequired    bool
   197→	SecurityReviewRequired bool
   198→	LineItems              []LineItem
   199→}
   200→
   201→// IntentContext carries runtime context used by policy rules.
   202→type IntentContext struct {
   203→	Environment      string
   204→	SourcePlatform   string
   205→	SourceSessionID  string
   206→	SourceTraceID    string
   207→	IPAddress        string
   208→	UserPresent      bool
   209→	RiskHints        []string
   210→	TemporaryGrantID string
   211→}
   212→
   213→// Disposition is the outcome of policy evaluation.
   214→type Disposition string
   215→
   216→const (
   217→	DispositionAllow               Disposition = "allow"
   218→	DispositionAllowModified       Disposition = "allow_modified"
   219→	DispositionRequireApproval     Disposition = "require_approval"
   220→	DispositionDeny                Disposition = "deny"
   221→	DispositionRequireMoreEvidence Disposition = "require_more_evidence"
   222→)
   223→
   224→// RiskLevel is the assessed risk of an intent.
   225→type RiskLevel string
   226→
   227→const (
   228→	RiskLevelLow      RiskLevel = "low"
   229→	RiskLevelMedium   RiskLevel = "medium"
   230→	RiskLevelHigh     RiskLevel = "high"
   231→	RiskLevelCritical RiskLevel = "critical"
   232→)
   233→
   234→// Decision is the output of policy evaluation.
   235→type Decision struct {
   236→	Disposition     Disposition
   237→	RiskLevel       RiskLevel
   238→	MatchedPolicies []PolicyMatch
   239→	DenialReason    string
   240→}
   241→
   242→// PolicyMatch records which policy rule produced a decision.
   243→type PolicyMatch struct {
   244→	PolicyID      string
   245→	PolicyVersion int
   246→	RuleID        string
   247→	Explanation   string
   248→}
   249→
   250→// EventType identifies the kind of audit event.
   251→type EventType string
   252→
   253→const (
   254→	EventTypeIntentSubmitted    EventType = "intent.submitted"
   255→	EventTypePolicyEvaluated    EventType = "policy.evaluated"
   256→	EventTypeApprovalRequested  EventType = "approval.requested"
   257→	EventTypeApprovalApproved   EventType = "approval.approved"
   258→	EventTypeApprovalDenied     EventType = "approval.denied"
   259→	EventTypeApprovalModified   EventType = "approval.modified"
   260→	EventTypeGrantIssued        EventType = "grant.issued"
   261→	EventTypeGrantRevoked       EventType = "grant.revoked"
   262→	EventTypeExecutionStarted   EventType = "execution.started"
   263→	EventTypeExecutionSucceeded EventType = "execution.succeeded"
   264→	EventTypeExecutionFailed    EventType = "execution.failed"
   265→)
   266→

input.content:
# Plan: Aileron MVP — Agent Integration & Core Lifecycle

## Context

Aileron has a running server, UI shell, and docs deployment, but all 21 non-health API endpoints return 501. The SPIs (policy, approval, connector, vault, audit, notify) are defined as Go interfaces with no implementations. It's time to bring the core lifecycle to life: **intent → policy → approval → grant → execution → trace**.

This plan recommends which agent to integrate first, which use case to start with, and the build sequence to reach a working end-to-end demo.

---

## Recommendation: Claude Code + Git/GitHub

### Why Claude Code (not OpenClaw)

- **You use it daily.** You're building this project with it right now — immediate dogfooding.
- **MCP is the cleanest integration.** Aileron exposes MCP tools (`submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`) that Claude Code calls natively. No shell-script glue or fragile hook parsing.
- **Hooks are a poor MVP fit.** PreToolUse hooks fire on every tool call, can't carry state (approval IDs) across turns, and require regex parsing of raw arguments. They're a policy bypass detector, not a policy engine integration.
- **OpenClaw adds framework learning cost without adding a user.** Claude Code gives you user zero on day one.

### Why Git/GitHub (not Calendar or Payments)

- **Immediate daily use.** Claude Code creates PRs constantly during normal work — you'll hit this flow every session.
- **Zero external cost.** No Stripe test mode, no Google OAuth dance. GitHub PATs are free and you already have one.
- **Exercises all core components.** Intent submission, policy evaluation ("is this PR to main?"), approval routing, grant issuance, execution via GitHub connector — the full lifecycle.
- **The connector stub already exists** at `core/connector/git/github/github.go` with the right action types wired.
- **Real stakes, safe to develop.** Wrong merge to production is operationally meaningful, but reversible — exactly the sweet spot for demonstrating why a control plane matters.

Payments is the most compelling conference demo, but Git gives the fastest path to a system you actually use. Payments is a natural Phase 2 once the lifecycle is proven.

---

## Integration Pattern: MCP Server

A Go binary (`cmd/aileron-mcp/`) that speaks MCP JSON-RPC over stdio. Claude Code connects via its MCP server config. The MCP server is a thin client that calls the Aileron HTTP API.

**MCP tools exposed:**

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `submit_intent` | Submit an action intent (PR create, etc). Returns disposition + approval_id or grant_id |
| `check_approval` | Poll approval status by ID. Returns status + grant_id if approved |
| `execute_action` | Execute a granted action. Returns execution result + PR URL |
| `list_pending_approvals` | List pending approvals for visibility |

**Claude Code config** (project-level `.mcp.json`):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "aileron": {
      "command": "./bin/aileron-mcp",
      "env": {
        "AILERON_API_URL": "http://localhost:8080",
        "AILERON_API_KEY": "dev"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Demo Scenario (What "Done" Looks Like)

1. Developer starts Aileron (`task up`). Server boots with seed policies.
2. Developer starts Claude Code with Aileron MCP server configured.
3. Developer asks: "Create a PR from fix/tax-rounding to main in acme/checkout"
4. Claude Code calls `submit_intent` MCP tool → Aileron evaluates policy → "require_approval" (PR targets main)
5. Claude Code tells user: "This PR requires approval. Review at http://localhost:3000/approvals/apr_xxx"
6. Developer opens approval UI, sees intent details + risk level, clicks **Approve**
7. Claude Code polls `check_approval`, sees `approved` with grant_id
8. Claude Code calls `execute_action` → Aileron injects GitHub PAT from vault → GitHub connector creates PR
9. Claude Code returns: "PR created: https://github.com/acme/checkout/pull/42"
10. Full event trace visible at `/traces`

**Auto-approve path:** PR to a feature branch → policy returns `allow` → grant issued immediately → no approval UI needed.

**Deny path:** Force push attempt → policy returns `deny` → Claude Code reports: "Denied per policy: force pushes not allowed."

---

## Build Sequence (7 Phases)

### Phase 1: In-Memory Storage Layer
**Goal:** Server holds state so endpoints return real data instead of 501.

**New package:** `core/store/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces (IntentStore, ApprovalStore, PolicyStore, GrantStore) |
| `core/store/mem/intent.go` | In-memory IntentStore (`sync.RWMutex` + map) |
| `core/store/mem/approval.go` | In-memory ApprovalStore |
| `core/store/mem/policy.go` | In-memory PolicyStore |
| `core/store/mem/grant.go` | In-memory GrantStore (execution grants) |
| `core/store/mem/audit.go` | In-memory audit.Store (append-only slice) |

- UUIDs generated server-side
- Store interfaces are persistence-layer; SPI interfaces are business-logic layer
- Same interfaces will get Postgres implementations later

### Phase 2: Policy Engine (Rule-Based)
**Goal:** Working `policy.Engine` that evaluates rules and returns decisions.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule model: conditions + disposition + risk level |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | `RuleEngine` implementing `policy.Engine` |

**Rule model:**
```go
Rule {
    ID, PolicyID    string
    Priority        int
    ActionTypePattern string     // glob: "git.*", "git.pull_request.create"
    Conditions      []Condition  // field matchers on intent/context
    Disposition     model.Disposition
    RiskLevel       model.RiskLevel
}
Condition {
    Field    string  // "domain.git.base_branch", "context.environment"
    Operator string  // "eq", "neq", "in", "matches"
    Value    any
}
```

**Evaluation:** Filter by action type pattern → evaluate conditions → highest-priority match wins → default `allow` if no match.

**Seed policies for demo:**
- `git.pull_request.create` where `base_branch in [main, production]` → `require_approval` (risk: medium)
- `git.pull_request.create` (catch-all) → `allow` (risk: low)
- `git.force_push` → `deny` (risk: critical)

### Phase 3: Approval Orchestrator
**Goal:** Working `approval.Orchestrator` that creates requests, tracks status, issues grants.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | `InMemoryOrchestrator` implementing `approval.Orchestrator` |

- `Request()` → creates approval with status `pending`, assigns default approver (workspace owner for MVP)
- `Approve()` → flips to `approved`, creates `ExecutionGrant` with 5-min TTL
- `Deny()` → flips to `denied`, records audit event
- `Modify()` → flips to `modified`, stores parameter constraints on grant

### Phase 4: Wire Core Lifecycle into API Endpoints
**Goal:** Replace all 501 stubs with real implementations.

**Modified:** `core/server/main.go` — `apiServer` struct gains fields for each SPI + store. Constructor wires everything.

**`CreateIntent` becomes the orchestration hub:**
1. Parse + validate request
2. Persist intent → emit `intent.submitted` audit event
3. Call `policy.Engine.Evaluate()`  → emit `policy.evaluated`
4. Based on disposition:
   - `allow` → create grant immediately, intent status `granted`
   - `require_approval` → call `orchestrator.Request()`, intent status `pending_approval`
   - `deny` → intent status `denied`
5. Return IntentEnvelope with status, decision, grant/approval ID

**Other endpoints:** Straightforward reads/writes through stores + SPI calls.

### Phase 5: GitHub Connector
**Goal:** `git.pull_request.create` actually creates a PR.

**Modified:** `core/connector/git/github/github.go`

- `createPullRequest`: Parse owner/repo, POST `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` with PAT from `req.Credential.Value`. Return PR URL as `ReceiptRef`.
- Raw `net/http` — no `go-github` dependency for MVP.

**Vault (MVP):** In-memory vault (`core/vault/mem.go`) that reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var at startup, stores at known path. Execution flow resolves credential before calling connector.

### Phase 6: MCP Server for Claude Code
**Goal:** Claude Code calls Aileron tools natively.

**New:** `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go`

- Go binary, MCP JSON-RPC over stdio
- Uses Go SDK (`sdk/go/`) to call Aileron HTTP API
- SDK needs TODO methods implemented (Create/Get/List for Intents, Approvals)
- Exposes 4 tools: `submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`, `list_pending_approvals`

### Phase 7: Approval UI
**Goal:** Humans approve/deny in the browser.

**New SvelteKit routes:**

| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/approvals` | List pending approvals (polls `GET /v1/approvals?status=pending`) |
| `/approvals/[id]` | Detail view: intent summary, policy decision, risk. Approve/Deny/Modify buttons |
| `/traces` | Audit log viewer |

No auth for MVP — runs locally.

---

## Parallelization

Once Phase 4 is complete, Phases 5, 6, and 7 can proceed in parallel:

```
Phase 1 (stores)
  ↓
Phase 2 (policy) + Phase 3 (approval)  ← parallel
  ↓
Phase 4 (wire endpoints)
  ↓
Phase 5 (GitHub connector) + Phase 6 (MCP server) + Phase 7 (approval UI)  ← parallel
```

---

## Key Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `core/server/main.go` | Inject SPI implementations + stores, replace 21 stub handlers |
| `core/connector/git/github/github.go` | Implement `createPullRequest` via GitHub REST API |
| `core/policy/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/approval/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/model/model.go` | No changes (types are ready) |

## New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces |
| `core/store/mem/*.go` | In-memory store implementations |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | Rule-based policy engine |
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule + condition models |
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | In-memory approval orchestrator |
| `core/vault/mem.go` | In-memory vault (env var backed) |
| `core/audit/mem.go` | In-memory audit store |
| `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go` | MCP server binary |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/+page.svelte` | Approval list page |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/[id]/+page.svelte` | Approval detail + action page |
| `ui/src/routes/traces/+page.svelte` | Audit trace viewer |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:** Policy engine evaluates seed rules correctly (allow/deny/require_approval)
2. **Integration test:** `POST /v1/intents` with a git.pull_request.create intent returns correct disposition
3. **End-to-end:** MCP server → submit_intent → approve in UI → execute → verify PR created on GitHub (test repo)
4. **Audit trail:** `GET /v1/traces` returns complete event history for the flow

| `core/store/mem/intent.go` | In-memory IntentStore (`sync.RWMutex` + map) |
| `core/store/mem/approval.go` | In-memory ApprovalStore |
| `core/store/mem/policy.go` | In-memory PolicyStore |
| `core/store/mem/grant.go` | In-memory GrantStore (execution grants) |
| `core/store/mem/audit.go` | In-memory audit.Store (append-only slice) |

- UUIDs generated server-side
- Store interfaces are persistence-layer; SPI interfaces are business-logic layer
- Same interfaces will get Postgres implementations later

### Phase 2: Policy Engine (Rule-Based)
**Goal:** Working `policy.Engine` that evaluates rules and returns decisions.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule model: conditions + disposition + risk level |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | `RuleEngine` implementing `policy.Engine` |

**Rule model:**
```go
Rule {
    ID, PolicyID    string
    Priority        int
    ActionTypePattern string     // glob: "git.*", "git.pull_request.create"
    Conditions      []Condition  // field matchers on intent/context
    Disposition     model.Disposition
    RiskLevel       model.RiskLevel
}
Condition {
    Field    string  // "domain.git.base_branch", "context.environment"
    Operator string  // "eq", "neq", "in", "matches"
    Value    any
}
```

**Evaluation:** Filter by action type pattern → evaluate conditions → highest-priority match wins → default `allow` if no match.

**Seed policies for demo:**
- `git.pull_request.create` where `base_branch in [main, production]` → `require_approval` (risk: medium)
- `git.pull_request.create` (catch-all) → `allow` (risk: low)
- `git.force_push` → `deny` (risk: critical)

### Phase 3: Approval Orchestrator
**Goal:** Working `approval.Orchestrator` that creates requests, tracks status, issues grants.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | `InMemoryOrchestrator` implementing `approval.Orchestrator` |

- `Request()` → creates approval with status `pending`, assigns default approver (workspace owner for MVP)
- `Approve()` → flips to `approved`, creates `ExecutionGrant` with 5-min TTL
- `Deny()` → flips to `denied`, records audit event
- `Modify()` → flips to `modified`, stores parameter constraints on grant

### Phase 4: Wire Core Lifecycle into API Endpoints
**Goal:** Replace all 501 stubs with real implementations.

**Modified:** `core/server/main.go` — `apiServer` struct gains fields for each SPI + store. Constructor wires everything.

**`CreateIntent` becomes the orchestration hub:**
1. Parse + validate request
2. Persist intent → emit `intent.submitted` audit event
3. Call `policy.Engine.Evaluate()`  → emit `policy.evaluated`
4. Based on disposition:
   - `allow` → create grant immediately, intent status `granted`
   - `require_approval` → call `orchestrator.Request()`, intent status `pending_approval`
   - `deny` → intent status `denied`
5. Return IntentEnvelope with status, decision, grant/approval ID

**Other endpoints:** Straightforward reads/writes through stores + SPI calls.

### Phase 5: GitHub Connector
**Goal:** `git.pull_request.create` actually creates a PR.

**Modified:** `core/connector/git/github/github.go`

- `createPullRequest`: Parse owner/repo, POST `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` with PAT from `req.Credential.Value`. Return PR URL as `ReceiptRef`.
- Raw `net/http` — no `go-github` dependency for MVP.

**Vault (MVP):** In-memory vault (`core/vault/mem.go`) that reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var at startup, stores at known path. Execution flow resolves credential before calling connector.

### Phase 6: MCP Server for Claude Code
**Goal:** Claude Code calls Aileron tools natively.

**New:** `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go`

- Go binary, MCP JSON-RPC over stdio
- Uses Go SDK (`sdk/go/`) to call Aileron HTTP API
- SDK needs TODO methods implemented (Create/Get/List for Intents, Approvals)
- Exposes 4 tools: `submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`, `list_pending_approvals`

### Phase 7: Approval UI
**Goal:** Humans approve/deny in the browser.

**New SvelteKit routes:**

| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/approvals` | List pending approvals (polls `GET /v1/approvals?status=pending`) |
| `/approvals/[id]` | Detail view: intent summary, policy decision, risk. Approve/Deny/Modify buttons |
| `/traces` | Audit log viewer |

No auth for MVP — runs locally.

---

## Parallelization

Once Phase 4 is complete, Phases 5, 6, and 7 can proceed in parallel:

```
Phase 1 (stores)
  ↓
Phase 2 (policy) + Phase 3 (approval)  ← parallel
  ↓
Phase 4 (wire endpoints)
  ↓
Phase 5 (GitHub connector) + Phase 6 (MCP server) + Phase 7 (approval UI)  ← parallel
```

---

## Key Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `core/server/main.go` | Inject SPI implementations + stores, replace 21 stub handlers |
| `core/connector/git/github/github.go` | Implement `createPullRequest` via GitHub REST API |
| `core/policy/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/approval/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/model/model.go` | No changes (types are ready) |

## New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces |
| `core/store/mem/*.go` | In-memory store implementations |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | Rule-based policy engine |
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule + condition models |
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | In-memory approval orchestrator |
| `core/vault/mem.go` | In-memory vault (env var backed) |
| `core/audit/mem.go` | In-memory audit store |
| `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go` | MCP server binary |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/+page.svelte` | Approval list page |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/[id]/+page.svelte` | Approval detail + action page |
| `ui/src/routes/traces/+page.svelte` | Audit trace viewer |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:** Policy engine evaluates seed rules correctly (allow/deny/require_approval)
2. **Integration test:** `POST /v1/intents` with a git.pull_request.create intent returns correct disposition
3. **End-to-end:** MCP server → submit_intent → approve in UI → execute → verify PR created on GitHub (test repo)
4. **Audit trail:** `GET /v1/traces` returns complete event history for the flow

Here's the summary of my recommendation:

**Agent: Claude Code via MCP Server.** You're already using it daily — immediate dogfooding. MCP gives a clean, stateful integration (tools like `submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`) without the fragility of hooks or learning a new agent framework like OpenClaw.

**Use case: Git/GitHub (PR creation).** It exercises the full lifecycle (intent → policy → approval → grant → execution → trace), costs nothing to test, and you'll hit the flow naturally during development. The connector stub already exists.

**Build sequence:** 7 phases — in-memory stores, policy engine, approval orchestrator, wire API endpoints, GitHub connector, MCP server, approval UI. Phases 2+3 parallelize, and phases 5+6+7 parallelize after 4.

# Plan: Aileron MVP — Agent Integration & Core Lifecycle

## Context

Aileron has a running server, UI shell, and docs deployment, but all 21 non-health API endpoints return 501. The SPIs (policy, approval, connector, vault, audit, notify) are defined as Go interfaces with no implementations. It's time to bring the core lifecycle to life: **intent → policy → approval → grant → execution → trace**.

This plan recommends which agent to integrate first, which use case to start with, and the build sequence to reach a working end-to-end demo.

---

## Recommendation: Claude Code + Git/GitHub

### Why Claude Code (not OpenClaw)

- **You use it daily.** You're building this project with it right now — immediate dogfooding.
- **MCP is the cleanest integration.** Aileron exposes MCP tools (`submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`) that Claude Code calls natively. No shell-script glue or fragile hook parsing.
- **Hooks are a poor MVP fit.** PreToolUse hooks fire on every tool call, can't carry state (approval IDs) across turns, and require regex parsing of raw arguments. They're a policy bypass detector, not a policy engine integration.
- **OpenClaw adds framework learning cost without adding a user.** Claude Code gives you user zero on day one.

### Why Git/GitHub (not Calendar or Payments)

- **Immediate daily use.** Claude Code creates PRs constantly during normal work — you'll hit this flow every session.
- **Zero external cost.** No Stripe test mode, no Google OAuth dance. GitHub PATs are free and you already have one.
- **Exercises all core components.** Intent submission, policy evaluation ("is this PR to main?"), approval routing, grant issuance, execution via GitHub connector — the full lifecycle.
- **The connector stub already exists** at `core/connector/git/github/github.go` with the right action types wired.
- **Real stakes, safe to develop.** Wrong merge to production is operationally meaningful, but reversible — exactly the sweet spot for demonstrating why a control plane matters.

Payments is the most compelling conference demo, but Git gives the fastest path to a system you actually use. Payments is a natural Phase 2 once the lifecycle is proven.

---

## Integration Pattern: MCP Server

A Go binary (`cmd/aileron-mcp/`) that speaks MCP JSON-RPC over stdio. Claude Code connects via its MCP server config. The MCP server is a thin client that calls the Aileron HTTP API.

**MCP tools exposed:**

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `submit_intent` | Submit an action intent (PR create, etc). Returns disposition + approval_id or grant_id |
| `check_approval` | Poll approval status by ID. Returns status + grant_id if approved |
| `execute_action` | Execute a granted action. Returns execution result + PR URL |
| `list_pending_approvals` | List pending approvals for visibility |

**Claude Code config** (project-level `.mcp.json`):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "aileron": {
      "command": "./bin/aileron-mcp",
      "env": {
        "AILERON_API_URL": "http://localhost:8080",
        "AILERON_API_KEY": "dev"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Demo Scenario (What "Done" Looks Like)

1. Developer starts Aileron (`task up`). Server boots with seed policies.
2. Developer starts Claude Code with Aileron MCP server configured.
3. Developer asks: "Create a PR from fix/tax-rounding to main in acme/checkout"
4. Claude Code calls `submit_intent` MCP tool → Aileron evaluates policy → "require_approval" (PR targets main)
5. Claude Code tells user: "This PR requires approval. Review at http://localhost:3000/approvals/apr_xxx"
6. Developer opens approval UI, sees intent details + risk level, clicks **Approve**
7. Claude Code polls `check_approval`, sees `approved` with grant_id
8. Claude Code calls `execute_action` → Aileron injects GitHub PAT from vault → GitHub connector creates PR
9. Claude Code returns: "PR created: https://github.com/acme/checkout/pull/42"
10. Full event trace visible at `/traces`

**Auto-approve path:** PR to a feature branch → policy returns `allow` → grant issued immediately → no approval UI needed.

**Deny path:** Force push attempt → policy returns `deny` → Claude Code reports: "Denied per policy: force pushes not allowed."

---

## Build Sequence (7 Phases)

### Phase 1: In-Memory Storage Layer
**Goal:** Server holds state so endpoints return real data instead of 501.

**New package:** `core/store/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces (IntentStore, ApprovalStore, PolicyStore, GrantStore) |
| `core/store/mem/intent.go` | In-memory IntentStore (`sync.RWMutex` + map) |
| `core/store/mem/approval.go` | In-memory ApprovalStore |
| `core/store/mem/policy.go` | In-memory PolicyStore |
| `core/store/mem/grant.go` | In-memory GrantStore (execution grants) |
| `core/store/mem/audit.go` | In-memory audit.Store (append-only slice) |

- UUIDs generated server-side
- Store interfaces are persistence-layer; SPI interfaces are business-logic layer
- Same interfaces will get Postgres implementations later

### Phase 2: Policy Engine (Rule-Based)
**Goal:** Working `policy.Engine` that evaluates rules and returns decisions.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule model: conditions + disposition + risk level |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | `RuleEngine` implementing `policy.Engine` |

**Rule model:**
```go
Rule {
    ID, PolicyID    string
    Priority        int
    ActionTypePattern string     // glob: "git.*", "git.pull_request.create"
    Conditions      []Condition  // field matchers on intent/context
    Disposition     model.Disposition
    RiskLevel       model.RiskLevel
}
Condition {
    Field    string  // "domain.git.base_branch", "context.environment"
    Operator string  // "eq", "neq", "in", "matches"
    Value    any
}
```

**Evaluation:** Filter by action type pattern → evaluate conditions → highest-priority match wins → default `allow` if no match.

**Seed policies for demo:**
- `git.pull_request.create` where `base_branch in [main, production]` → `require_approval` (risk: medium)
- `git.pull_request.create` (catch-all) → `allow` (risk: low)
- `git.force_push` → `deny` (risk: critical)

### Phase 3: Approval Orchestrator
**Goal:** Working `approval.Orchestrator` that creates requests, tracks status, issues grants.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | `InMemoryOrchestrator` implementing `approval.Orchestrator` |

- `Request()` → creates approval with status `pending`, assigns default approver (workspace owner for MVP)
- `Approve()` → flips to `approved`, creates `ExecutionGrant` with 5-min TTL
- `Deny()` → flips to `denied`, records audit event
- `Modify()` → flips to `modified`, stores parameter constraints on grant

### Phase 4: Wire Core Lifecycle into API Endpoints
**Goal:** Replace all 501 stubs with real implementations.

**Modified:** `core/server/main.go` — `apiServer` struct gains fields for each SPI + store. Constructor wires everything.

**`CreateIntent` becomes the orchestration hub:**
1. Parse + validate request
2. Persist intent → emit `intent.submitted` audit event
3. Call `policy.Engine.Evaluate()`  → emit `policy.evaluated`
4. Based on disposition:
   - `allow` → create grant immediately, intent status `granted`
   - `require_approval` → call `orchestrator.Request()`, intent status `pending_approval`
   - `deny` → intent status `denied`
5. Return IntentEnvelope with status, decision, grant/approval ID

**Other endpoints:** Straightforward reads/writes through stores + SPI calls.

### Phase 5: GitHub Connector
**Goal:** `git.pull_request.create` actually creates a PR.

**Modified:** `core/connector/git/github/github.go`

- `createPullRequest`: Parse owner/repo, POST `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` with PAT from `req.Credential.Value`. Return PR URL as `ReceiptRef`.
- Raw `net/http` — no `go-github` dependency for MVP.

**Vault (MVP):** In-memory vault (`core/vault/mem.go`) that reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var at startup, stores at known path. Execution flow resolves credential before calling connector.

### Phase 6: MCP Server for Claude Code
**Goal:** Claude Code calls Aileron tools natively.

**New:** `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go`

- Go binary, MCP JSON-RPC over stdio
- Uses Go SDK (`sdk/go/`) to call Aileron HTTP API
- SDK needs TODO methods implemented (Create/Get/List for Intents, Approvals)
- Exposes 4 tools: `submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`, `list_pending_approvals`

### Phase 7: Approval UI
**Goal:** Humans approve/deny in the browser.

**New SvelteKit routes:**

| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/approvals` | List pending approvals (polls `GET /v1/approvals?status=pending`) |
| `/approvals/[id]` | Detail view: intent summary, policy decision, risk. Approve/Deny/Modify buttons |
| `/traces` | Audit log viewer |

No auth for MVP — runs locally.

---

## Parallelization

Once Phase 4 is complete, Phases 5, 6, and 7 can proceed in parallel:

```
Phase 1 (stores)
  ↓
Phase 2 (policy) + Phase 3 (approval)  ← parallel
  ↓
Phase 4 (wire endpoints)
  ↓
Phase 5 (GitHub connector) + Phase 6 (MCP server) + Phase 7 (approval UI)  ← parallel
```

---

## Key Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `core/server/main.go` | Inject SPI implementations + stores, replace 21 stub handlers |
| `core/connector/git/github/github.go` | Implement `createPullRequest` via GitHub REST API |
| `core/policy/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/approval/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/model/model.go` | No changes (types are ready) |

## New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces |
| `core/store/mem/*.go` | In-memory store implementations |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | Rule-based policy engine |
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule + condition models |
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | In-memory approval orchestrator |
| `core/vault/mem.go` | In-memory vault (env var backed) |
| `core/audit/mem.go` | In-memory audit store |
| `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go` | MCP server binary |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/+page.svelte` | Approval list page |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/[id]/+page.svelte` | Approval detail + action page |
| `ui/src/routes/traces/+page.svelte` | Audit trace viewer |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:** Policy engine evaluates seed rules correctly (allow/deny/require_approval)
2. **Integration test:** `POST /v1/intents` with a git.pull_request.create intent returns correct disposition
3. **End-to-end:** MCP server → submit_intent → approve in UI → execute → verify PR created on GitHub (test repo)
4. **Audit trail:** `GET /v1/traces` returns complete event history for the flow

| `/approvals/[id]` | Detail view: intent summary, policy decision, risk. Approve/Deny/Modify buttons |
| `/traces` | Audit log viewer |

No auth for MVP — runs locally.

---

## Parallelization

Once Phase 4 is complete, Phases 5, 6, and 7 can proceed in parallel:

```
Phase 1 (stores)
  ↓
Phase 2 (policy) + Phase 3 (approval)  ← parallel
  ↓
Phase 4 (wire endpoints)
  ↓
Phase 5 (GitHub connector) + Phase 6 (MCP server) + Phase 7 (approval UI)  ← parallel
```

---

## Key Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `core/server/main.go` | Inject SPI implementations + stores, replace 21 stub handlers |
| `core/connector/git/github/github.go` | Implement `createPullRequest` via GitHub REST API |
| `core/policy/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/approval/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/model/model.go` | No changes (types are ready) |

## New Files

| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces |

| `ui/src/routes/traces/+page.svelte` | Audit trace viewer |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:** Policy engine evaluates seed rules correctly (allow/deny/require_approval)
2. **Integration test:** `POST /v1/intents` with a git.pull_request.create intent returns correct disposition
3. **End-to-end:** MCP server → submit_intent → approve in UI → execute → verify PR created on GitHub (test repo)
4. **Audit trail:** `GET /v1/traces` returns complete event history for the flow

toolUseResult.plan:
# Plan: Aileron MVP — Agent Integration & Core Lifecycle

## Context

Aileron has a running server, UI shell, and docs deployment, but all 21 non-health API endpoints return 501. The SPIs (policy, approval, connector, vault, audit, notify) are defined as Go interfaces with no implementations. It's time to bring the core lifecycle to life: **intent → policy → approval → grant → execution → trace**.

This plan recommends which agent to integrate first, which use case to start with, and the build sequence to reach a working end-to-end demo.

---

## Recommendation: Claude Code + Git/GitHub

### Why Claude Code (not OpenClaw)

- **You use it daily.** You're building this project with it right now — immediate dogfooding.
- **MCP is the cleanest integration.** Aileron exposes MCP tools (`submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`) that Claude Code calls natively. No shell-script glue or fragile hook parsing.
- **Hooks are a poor MVP fit.** PreToolUse hooks fire on every tool call, can't carry state (approval IDs) across turns, and require regex parsing of raw arguments. They're a policy bypass detector, not a policy engine integration.
- **OpenClaw adds framework learning cost without adding a user.** Claude Code gives you user zero on day one.

### Why Git/GitHub (not Calendar or Payments)

- **Immediate daily use.** Claude Code creates PRs constantly during normal work — you'll hit this flow every session.
- **Zero external cost.** No Stripe test mode, no Google OAuth dance. GitHub PATs are free and you already have one.
- **Exercises all core components.** Intent submission, policy evaluation ("is this PR to main?"), approval routing, grant issuance, execution via GitHub connector — the full lifecycle.
- **The connector stub already exists** at `core/connector/git/github/github.go` with the right action types wired.
- **Real stakes, safe to develop.** Wrong merge to production is operationally meaningful, but reversible — exactly the sweet spot for demonstrating why a control plane matters.

Payments is the most compelling conference demo, but Git gives the fastest path to a system you actually use. Payments is a natural Phase 2 once the lifecycle is proven.

---

## Integration Pattern: MCP Server

A Go binary (`cmd/aileron-mcp/`) that speaks MCP JSON-RPC over stdio. Claude Code connects via its MCP server config. The MCP server is a thin client that calls the Aileron HTTP API.

**MCP tools exposed:**

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `submit_intent` | Submit an action intent (PR create, etc). Returns disposition + approval_id or grant_id |
| `check_approval` | Poll approval status by ID. Returns status + grant_id if approved |
| `execute_action` | Execute a granted action. Returns execution result + PR URL |
| `list_pending_approvals` | List pending approvals for visibility |

**Claude Code config** (project-level `.mcp.json`):
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "aileron": {
      "command": "./bin/aileron-mcp",
      "env": {
        "AILERON_API_URL": "http://localhost:8080",
        "AILERON_API_KEY": "dev"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Demo Scenario (What "Done" Looks Like)

1. Developer starts Aileron (`task up`). Server boots with seed policies.
2. Developer starts Claude Code with Aileron MCP server configured.
3. Developer asks: "Create a PR from fix/tax-rounding to main in acme/checkout"
4. Claude Code calls `submit_intent` MCP tool → Aileron evaluates policy → "require_approval" (PR targets main)
5. Claude Code tells user: "This PR requires approval. Review at http://localhost:3000/approvals/apr_xxx"
6. Developer opens approval UI, sees intent details + risk level, clicks **Approve**
7. Claude Code polls `check_approval`, sees `approved` with grant_id
8. Claude Code calls `execute_action` → Aileron injects GitHub PAT from vault → GitHub connector creates PR
9. Claude Code returns: "PR created: https://github.com/acme/checkout/pull/42"
10. Full event trace visible at `/traces`

**Auto-approve path:** PR to a feature branch → policy returns `allow` → grant issued immediately → no approval UI needed.

**Deny path:** Force push attempt → policy returns `deny` → Claude Code reports: "Denied per policy: force pushes not allowed."

---

## Build Sequence (7 Phases)

### Phase 1: In-Memory Storage Layer
**Goal:** Server holds state so endpoints return real data instead of 501.

**New package:** `core/store/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces (IntentStore, ApprovalStore, PolicyStore, GrantStore) |
| `core/store/mem/intent.go` | In-memory IntentStore (`sync.RWMutex` + map) |
| `core/store/mem/approval.go` | In-memory ApprovalStore |
| `core/store/mem/policy.go` | In-memory PolicyStore |
| `core/store/mem/grant.go` | In-memory GrantStore (execution grants) |
| `core/store/mem/audit.go` | In-memory audit.Store (append-only slice) |

- UUIDs generated server-side
- Store interfaces are persistence-layer; SPI interfaces are business-logic layer
- Same interfaces will get Postgres implementations later

### Phase 2: Policy Engine (Rule-Based)
**Goal:** Working `policy.Engine` that evaluates rules and returns decisions.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule model: conditions + disposition + risk level |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | `RuleEngine` implementing `policy.Engine` |

**Rule model:**
```go
Rule {
    ID, PolicyID    string
    Priority        int
    ActionTypePattern string     // glob: "git.*", "git.pull_request.create"
    Conditions      []Condition  // field matchers on intent/context
    Disposition     model.Disposition
    RiskLevel       model.RiskLevel
}
Condition {
    Field    string  // "domain.git.base_branch", "context.environment"
    Operator string  // "eq", "neq", "in", "matches"
    Value    any
}
```

**Evaluation:** Filter by action type pattern → evaluate conditions → highest-priority match wins → default `allow` if no match.

**Seed policies for demo:**
- `git.pull_request.create` where `base_branch in [main, production]` → `require_approval` (risk: medium)
- `git.pull_request.create` (catch-all) → `allow` (risk: low)
- `git.force_push` → `deny` (risk: critical)

### Phase 3: Approval Orchestrator
**Goal:** Working `approval.Orchestrator` that creates requests, tracks status, issues grants.

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | `InMemoryOrchestrator` implementing `approval.Orchestrator` |

- `Request()` → creates approval with status `pending`, assigns default approver (workspace owner for MVP)
- `Approve()` → flips to `approved`, creates `ExecutionGrant` with 5-min TTL
- `Deny()` → flips to `denied`, records audit event
- `Modify()` → flips to `modified`, stores parameter constraints on grant

### Phase 4: Wire Core Lifecycle into API Endpoints
**Goal:** Replace all 501 stubs with real implementations.

**Modified:** `core/server/main.go` — `apiServer` struct gains fields for each SPI + store. Constructor wires everything.

**`CreateIntent` becomes the orchestration hub:**
1. Parse + validate request
2. Persist intent → emit `intent.submitted` audit event
3. Call `policy.Engine.Evaluate()`  → emit `policy.evaluated`
4. Based on disposition:
   - `allow` → create grant immediately, intent status `granted`
   - `require_approval` → call `orchestrator.Request()`, intent status `pending_approval`
   - `deny` → intent status `denied`
5. Return IntentEnvelope with status, decision, grant/approval ID

**Other endpoints:** Straightforward reads/writes through stores + SPI calls.

### Phase 5: GitHub Connector
**Goal:** `git.pull_request.create` actually creates a PR.

**Modified:** `core/connector/git/github/github.go`

- `createPullRequest`: Parse owner/repo, POST `/repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls` with PAT from `req.Credential.Value`. Return PR URL as `ReceiptRef`.
- Raw `net/http` — no `go-github` dependency for MVP.

**Vault (MVP):** In-memory vault (`core/vault/mem.go`) that reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var at startup, stores at known path. Execution flow resolves credential before calling connector.

### Phase 6: MCP Server for Claude Code
**Goal:** Claude Code calls Aileron tools natively.

**New:** `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go`

- Go binary, MCP JSON-RPC over stdio
- Uses Go SDK (`sdk/go/`) to call Aileron HTTP API
- SDK needs TODO methods implemented (Create/Get/List for Intents, Approvals)
- Exposes 4 tools: `submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`, `list_pending_approvals`

### Phase 7: Approval UI
**Goal:** Humans approve/deny in the browser.

**New SvelteKit routes:**

| Route | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/approvals` | List pending approvals (polls `GET /v1/approvals?status=pending`) |
| `/approvals/[id]` | Detail view: intent summary, policy decision, risk. Approve/Deny/Modify buttons |
| `/traces` | Audit log viewer |

No auth for MVP — runs locally.

---

## Parallelization

Once Phase 4 is complete, Phases 5, 6, and 7 can proceed in parallel:

```
Phase 1 (stores)
  ↓
Phase 2 (policy) + Phase 3 (approval)  ← parallel
  ↓
Phase 4 (wire endpoints)
  ↓
Phase 5 (GitHub connector) + Phase 6 (MCP server) + Phase 7 (approval UI)  ← parallel
```

---

## Key Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `core/server/main.go` | Inject SPI implementations + stores, replace 21 stub handlers |
| `core/connector/git/github/github.go` | Implement `createPullRequest` via GitHub REST API |
| `core/policy/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/approval/spi.go` | No changes (interface is ready) |
| `core/model/model.go` | No changes (types are ready) |

## New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/store/store.go` | Store interfaces |
| `core/store/mem/*.go` | In-memory store implementations |
| `core/policy/engine.go` | Rule-based policy engine |
| `core/policy/rule.go` | Rule + condition models |
| `core/approval/orchestrator.go` | In-memory approval orchestrator |
| `core/vault/mem.go` | In-memory vault (env var backed) |
| `core/audit/mem.go` | In-memory audit store |
| `cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go` | MCP server binary |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/+page.svelte` | Approval list page |
| `ui/src/routes/approvals/[id]/+page.svelte` | Approval detail + action page |
| `ui/src/routes/traces/+page.svelte` | Audit trace viewer |

---

## Verification

1. **Unit tests:** Policy engine evaluates seed rules correctly (allow/deny/require_approval)
2. **Integration test:** `POST /v1/intents` with a git.pull_request.create intent returns correct disposition
3. **End-to-end:** MCP server → submit_intent → approve in UI → execute → verify PR created on GitHub (test repo)
4. **Audit trail:** `GET /v1/traces` returns complete event history for the flow

1→// Package vault defines the SPI for credential and secret storage.
     2→//
     3→// The vault stores sensitive values (API keys, OAuth tokens, service account
     4→// credentials) and injects them into connector execution requests without
     5→// ever exposing them to agents. Agents see only a credential reference ID;
     6→// the vault resolves the actual secret at execution time.
     7→//
     8→// The built-in implementation encrypts secrets at rest using AES-GCM with
     9→// a key stored in the environment. Alternative implementations can delegate
    10→// to HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager, etc.
    11→package vault
    12→
    13→import "context"
    14→
    15→// Vault stores and retrieves secrets by path.
    16→type Vault interface {
    17→	// Get retrieves a secret by its vault path.
    18→	Get(ctx context.Context, path string) (Secret, error)
    19→
    20→	// Put stores a secret at the given path, creating or overwriting it.
    21→	Put(ctx context.Context, path string, value []byte, meta Metadata) error
    22→
    23→	// Delete permanently removes a secret.
    24→	Delete(ctx context.Context, path string) error
    25→}
    26→
    27→// Secret is a resolved secret value.
    28→type Secret struct {
    29→	Path     string
    30→	Value    []byte
    31→	Metadata Metadata
    32→}
    33→
    34→// Metadata carries non-secret attributes stored alongside a secret.
    35→type Metadata struct {
    36→	// Type classifies the credential (e.g. "api_key", "oauth_refresh_token").
    37→	Type        string
    38→	Environment string
    39→	// Labels are arbitrary key-value pairs for organization.
    40→	Labels map[string]string
    41→}
    42→

1→// Package audit defines the SPI for the immutable audit/trace store.
     2→//
     3→// Every significant event in the control plane lifecycle — intent submission,
     4→// policy decision, approval action, execution outcome — is recorded as an
     5→// append-only trace event. This provides a verifiable audit trail for
     6→// compliance, debugging, and trust.
     7→//
     8→// The built-in implementation writes to a Postgres append-only table.
     9→// Alternative implementations can target immutable object storage, a
    10→// dedicated audit log service, or a blockchain-backed store.
    11→package audit
    12→
    13→import (
    14→	"context"
    15→	"time"
    16→
    17→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/model"
    18→)
    19→
    20→// Store records and retrieves immutable trace events.
    21→type Store interface {
    22→	// Append records a new event. Implementations must treat this as
    23→	// append-only: existing events must never be modified or deleted.
    24→	Append(ctx context.Context, event Event) error
    25→
    26→	// ListTraces returns traces matching the filter.
    27→	ListTraces(ctx context.Context, filter Filter) ([]Trace, error)
    28→
    29→	// GetTrace returns the full trace for a given intent.
    30→	GetTrace(ctx context.Context, traceID string) (Trace, error)
    31→}
    32→
    33→// Event is a single immutable audit record.
    34→type Event struct {
    35→	EventID   string
    36→	EventType model.EventType
    37→	Actor     model.ActorRef
    38→	// Payload carries event-specific data. Must be serialisable to JSON.
    39→	Payload   map[string]any
    40→	Timestamp time.Time
    41→}
    42→
    43→// Trace is the full ordered event history for one intent.
    44→type Trace struct {
    45→	TraceID     string
    46→	IntentID    string
    47→	WorkspaceID string
    48→	Events      []Event
    49→}
    50→
    51→// Filter scopes a trace list query.
    52→type Filter struct {
    53→	WorkspaceID string
    54→	IntentID    string
    55→	ActorID     string
    56→	EventType   model.EventType
    57→	From        *time.Time
    58→	To          *time.Time
    59→	PageSize    int
    60→	PageToken   string
    61→}
    62→

200→		return false
   201→	}
   202→}

1036→type ActionIntent struct {
  1037→	// Domain Carries action-type-specific fields. Exactly one field must be populated,
  1038→	// corresponding to the prefix of ActionIntent.type:
  1039→	//   - git.* → git
  1040→	//   - deploy.* → deploy
  1041→	//   - cloud.* → cloud
  1042→	//   - email.* → email

     3→// This connector executes git.pull_request.* and git.commit.* actions via

    34→// Execute implements connector.Connector.

    36→	if req.Credential == nil {
    37→		return connector.ExecutionResult{}, errors.New("github: no credential injected")
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	switch req.ActionType {
    41→	case "git.pull_request.create":
    42→		return c.createPullRequest(ctx, req)
    43→	case "git.pull_request.merge":

    45→	case "git.pull_request.close":

    47→	default:
    48→		return connector.ExecutionResult{
    49→			Status: connector.ExecutionStatusFailed,
    50→			Error:  "github: unsupported action type: " + req.ActionType,
    51→		}, nil
    52→	}
    53→}

file.content:
version: "3"

vars:
  COMPOSE_FILE: deploy/docker-compose.yml

tasks:
  default:
    desc: List available tasks
    cmds:
      - task --list

  generate:
    desc: Run all code generators
    cmds:
      - task: generate:api

  generate:api:
    desc: Generate Go types and server interface from OpenAPI spec
    dir: core
    cmds:
      - go run github.com/oapi-codegen/oapi-codegen/v2/cmd/oapi-codegen --config api/oapi-codegen.yaml api/openapi.yaml
    sources:
      - api/openapi.yaml
      - api/oapi-codegen.yaml
    generates:
      - api/gen/server.gen.go

  build:
    desc: Build all containers
    cmds:
      - docker compose -f {{.COMPOSE_FILE}} build

  build:server:
    desc: Build the Go server binary locally
    cmds:
      - go build -o server ./core/server

  build:ui:
    desc: Install UI dependencies and build
    dir: ui
    cmds:
      - pnpm install
      - pnpm run build

  up:
    desc: Start all services via Docker Compose
    cmds:
      - docker compose -f {{.COMPOSE_FILE}} up {{.CLI_ARGS}}

  down:
    desc: Stop all services
    cmds:
      - docker compose -f {{.COMPOSE_FILE}} down {{.CLI_ARGS}}

  clean:
    desc: Stop services and remove volumes
    cmds:
      - docker compose -f {{.COMPOSE_FILE}} down -v --remove-orphans
      - rm -f server
      - rm -rf ui/.svelte-kit ui/build ui/node_modules

  logs:
    desc: Tail service logs
    cmds:
      - docker compose -f {{.COMPOSE_FILE}} logs -f {{.CLI_ARGS}}

  ps:
    desc: Show running services
    cmds:
      - docker compose -f {{.COMPOSE_FILE}} ps

  dev:ui:
    desc: Run the UI dev server locally
    dir: ui
    cmds:
      - pnpm dev

  lint:
    desc: Run linters
    cmds:
      - task: lint:go
      - task: lint:ui

  lint:go:
    desc: Vet Go code
    cmds:

content.text:
Everything is clean. Here's what was built across all 7 phases:

## Summary

### Phase 1: In-Memory Storage Layer
- **`core/store/store.go`** — Store interfaces for all entity types (Intent, Approval, Policy, Grant, Execution, Connector, Credential, FundingSource) + `ErrNotFound`
- **`core/store/mem/*.go`** — 8 in-memory implementations with `sync.RWMutex` thread safety
- **`core/vault/mem.go`** — In-memory vault (reads `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var at startup)
- **`core/notify/log.go`** — Log-only notifier for MVP

### Phase 2: Rule-Based Policy Engine
- **`core/policy/engine.go`** — `RuleEngine` implementing `policy.Engine` with condition evaluation, action type matching, priority sorting, and field flattening for git/payment/calendar domains
- **`core/policy/seed.go`** — 3 seed policies: require approval for PRs to main, allow feature branch PRs, deny force pushes

### Phase 3: Approval Orchestrator
- **`core/approval/orchestrator.go`** — `InMemoryOrchestrator` with Request/Approve/Deny/Modify/List lifecycle
- **`core/approval/convert.go`** — Bidirectional conversion between SPI and API types

### Phase 4: Wired API Endpoints
- **`core/server/main.go`** — Rewired to inject all SPI implementations + stores, seed policies on startup
- **`core/server/handlers.go`** — All 22 endpoints implemented: `CreateIntent` as the orchestration hub (intent → policy → approval/grant → trace), plus CRUD for all entity types, `RunExecution` with vault credential injection, and model conversion helpers

### Phase 5: GitHub Connector
- **`core/connector/git/github/github.go`** — Full implementation of `createPullRequest`, `mergePullRequest`, `closePullRequest` via GitHub REST API using raw `net/http`

### Phase 6: MCP Server
- **`cmd/aileron-mcp/main.go`** — MCP server binary (stdio JSON-RPC) exposing 4 tools: `submit_intent`, `check_approval`, `execute_action`, `list_pending_approvals`
- **`sdk/go/client.go`** — Implemented SDK methods (Intents.Create/Get, Approvals.Get/List, Executions.Run/Get)
- Updated `go.work` and `Taskfile.yml`

### Phase 7: Approval UI
- **`ui/src/routes/+layout.svelte`** — Navigation with dark theme
- **`ui/src/routes/+page.svelte`** — Dashboard with cards linking to Approvals/Traces/Policies
- **`ui/src/routes/approvals/+page.svelte`** — Approval list with auto-refresh polling
- **`ui/src/routes/approvals/[id]/+page.svelte`** — Approval detail with intent info, risk level, policy matches, and Approve/Deny buttons
- **`ui/src/routes/traces/+page.svelte`** — Audit trace viewer with expandable event timelines
- **`ui/src/routes/policies/+page.svelte`** — Policy viewer showing rules and conditions
- **`ui/src/lib/api.ts`** — API client for the SvelteKit frontend

1→//go:build integration
     2→
     3→package integration
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"runtime"
    11→	"sync"
    12→	"testing"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/openapi3"
    15→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/openapi3filter"
    16→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/routers"
    17→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/routers/gorillamux"
    18→)
    19→
    20→var (
    21→	specRouter routers.Router
    22→	specOnce   sync.Once
    23→	specErr    error
    24→)
    25→
    26→func loadRouter() (routers.Router, error) {
    27→	specOnce.Do(func() {
    28→		specPath := specFilePath()
    29→		loader := openapi3.NewLoader()
    30→		doc, err := loader.LoadFromFile(specPath)
    31→		if err != nil {
    32→			specErr = err
    33→			return
    34→		}
    35→		// Clear servers so validation doesn't check host/port.
    36→		doc.Servers = nil
    37→		// Skip doc.Validate() — kin-openapi's validator does not fully
    38→		// support OpenAPI 3.1 and rejects valid 3.1 keywords like
    39→		// schema-level "examples". Response validation still works.
    40→		specRouter, specErr = gorillamux.NewRouter(doc)
    41→	})
    42→	return specRouter, specErr
    43→}
    44→
    45→// specFilePath returns the absolute path to the OpenAPI spec.
    46→func specFilePath() string {
    47→	if p := os.Getenv("AILERON_OPENAPI_SPEC"); p != "" {
    48→		return p
    49→	}
    50→	// Default: relative to this source file -> ../../core/api/openapi.yaml
    51→	_, thisFile, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
    52→	return filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(thisFile), "..", "..", "core", "api", "openapi.yaml")
    53→}
    54→
    55→// validateResponse checks that an HTTP response conforms to the OpenAPI spec.
    56→func validateResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {
    57→	t.Helper()
    58→
    59→	router, err := loadRouter()
    60→	if err != nil {
    61→		t.Fatalf("failed to load OpenAPI spec: %v", err)
    62→	}
    63→
    64→	route, pathParams, err := router.FindRoute(resp.Request)
    65→	if err != nil {
    66→		t.Fatalf("no matching route in spec for %s %s: %v", resp.Request.Method, resp.Request.URL.Path, err)
    67→	}
    68→
    69→	input := &openapi3filter.ResponseValidationInput{
    70→		RequestValidationInput: &openapi3filter.RequestValidationInput{
    71→			Request:    resp.Request,
    72→			PathParams: pathParams,
    73→			Route:      route,
    74→		},
    75→		Status: resp.StatusCode,
    76→		Header: resp.Header,
    77→		Body:   resp.Body,
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	if err := openapi3filter.ValidateResponse(context.Background(), input); err != nil {
    81→		t.Errorf("response does not match OpenAPI spec: %v", err)
    82→	}
    83→}
    84→

     4→  title: Agentic Control Plane API
     5→  version: 0.2.0

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"encoding/json"
     6→	"errors"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"net/http"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
    12→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/approval"
    13→	connectorpkg "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector"
    14→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/model"
    15→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/notify"
    16→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/policy"
    17→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store"
    18→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem"
    19→)
    20→
    21→// --- JSON helpers ---
    22→
    23→func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) {
    24→	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    25→	w.WriteHeader(status)
    26→	json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v)
    27→}
    28→
    29→func writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, code, message string) {
    30→	writeJSON(w, status, api.Error{
    31→		Error: struct {
    32→			Code      string                    `json:"code"`
    33→			Details   *[]map[string]interface{} `json:"details,omitempty"`
    34→			Message   string                    `json:"message"`
    35→			RequestId *string                   `json:"request_id,omitempty"`
    36→		}{
    37→			Code:    code,
    38→			Message: message,
    39→		},
    40→	})
    41→}
    42→
    43→func decodeBody(r *http.Request, v any) error {
    44→	defer r.Body.Close()
    45→	return json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(v)
    46→}
    47→
    48→func isNotFound(err error) bool {
    49→	var nf *store.ErrNotFound
    50→	return errors.As(err, &nf)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// --- Health ---
    54→
    55→func (s *apiServer) GetHealth(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    56→	writeJSON(w, http.StatusOK, api.HealthResponse{
    57→		Status:    "ok",
    58→		Service:   "aileron",
    59→		Version:   "0.2.0",
    60→		Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
    61→	})
    62→}
    63→
    64→// --- Intents ---
    65→
    66→func (s *apiServer) CreateIntent(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    67→	var req api.CreateIntentRequest
    68→	if err := decodeBody(r, &req); err != nil {
    69→		writeError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "invalid_request", err.Error())
    70→		return
    71→	}
    72→
    73→	ctx := r.Context()
    74→	now := time.Now().UTC()
    75→	intentID := "int_" + s.newID()
    76→	traceID := "trc_" + s.newID()
    77→
    78→	// Build the intent envelope.
    79→	envelope := api.IntentEnvelope{
    80→		IntentId:    intentID,
    81→		WorkspaceId: req.WorkspaceId,
    82→		Agent: api.ActorRef{
    83→			Id:   req.AgentId,
    84→			Type: api.Agent,
    85→		},
    86→		Action:    req.Action,
    87→		Context:   req.Context,
    88→		Status:    api.PendingPolicy,
    89→		CreatedAt: now,
    90→		UpdatedAt: now,
    91→		Decision: api.Decision{
    92→			Disposition: api.DecisionDispositionAllow,
    93→			RiskLevel:   api.Low,
    94→		},
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	if err := s.intents.Create(ctx, envelope); err != nil {
    98→		writeError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "store_error", err.Error())
    99→		return
   100→	}

1→// Package main is the entry point for the Aileron control plane server.
     2→//
     3→// The server wires together the core components — policy engine, approval
     4→// orchestrator, connector registry, vault, notifiers, and audit store —
     5→// and exposes the control plane API over HTTP.
     6→package main
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"context"
    10→	"log/slog"
    11→	"net/http"
    12→	"os"
    13→	"os/signal"
    14→	"syscall"
    15→	"time"
    16→
    17→	api "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/gen"
    18→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/approval"
    19→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector"
    20→	googlecalendar "github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/calendar/google"
    21→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/git/github"
    22→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/connector/payments/stripe"
    23→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/notify"
    24→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/policy"
    25→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/store/mem"
    26→	"github.com/ALRubinger/aileron/core/vault"
    27→	"github.com/google/uuid"
    28→)
    29→
    30→func main() {
    31→	log := slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, nil))
    32→
    33→	if err := run(log); err != nil {
    34→		log.Error("server exited with error", "error", err)
    35→		os.Exit(1)
    36→	}
    37→}
    38→
    39→func run(log *slog.Logger) error {
    40→	ctx := context.Background()
    41→	cfg := configFromEnv()
    42→
    43→	// --- In-memory stores ---
    44→	intentStore := mem.NewIntentStore()
    45→	approvalStore := mem.NewApprovalStore()
    46→	policyStore := mem.NewPolicyStore()
    47→	grantStore := mem.NewGrantStore()
    48→	executionStore := mem.NewExecutionStore()
    49→	connectorStore := mem.NewConnectorStore()
    50→	credentialStore := mem.NewCredentialStore()
    51→	fundingSourceStore := mem.NewFundingSourceStore()
    52→	traceStore := mem.NewTraceStore()
    53→
    54→	// --- Connector registry ---
    55→	registry := connector.NewRegistry()
    56→	registry.Register(ctx, stripe.New())
    57→	registry.Register(ctx, googlecalendar.New())
    58→	registry.Register(ctx, github.New())
    59→
    60→	// --- Vault ---
    61→	v := vault.NewMemVault()
    62→	// Seed GitHub PAT from environment if available.
    63→	if token := os.Getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN"); token != "" {
    64→		v.Put(ctx, "connectors/github/default", []byte(token), vault.Metadata{
    65→			Type: "api_key",
    66→			Labels: map[string]string{
    67→				"connector": "github",
    68→			},
    69→		})
    70→		log.Info("seeded GitHub token into vault")
    71→	}
    72→
    73→	// --- Policy engine ---
    74→	policyEngine := policy.NewRuleEngine(policyStore)
    75→
    76→	// Seed default policies.
    77→	if err := policy.SeedPolicies(ctx, policyStore); err != nil {
    78→		return err
    79→	}
    80→	log.Info("seeded default policies")
    81→
    82→	// --- Approval orchestrator ---
    83→	idGen := func() string { return uuid.New().String() }
    84→	orchestrator := approval.NewInMemoryOrchestrator(approvalStore, idGen)
    85→
    86→	// --- Notifier ---
    87→	notifier := notify.NewLogNotifier(log)
    88→
    89→	// --- HTTP server ---
    90→	mux := http.NewServeMux()
    91→
    92→	server := &apiServer{
    93→		log:            log,
    94→		registry:       registry,
    95→		policyEngine:   policyEngine,
    96→		orchestrator:   orchestrator,
    97→		vault:          v,
    98→		notifier:       notifier,
    99→		intents:        intentStore,
   100→		approvals:      approvalStore,
   101→		policies:       policyStore,
   102→		grants:         grantStore,
   103→		executions:     executionStore,
   104→		connectors:     connectorStore,
   105→		credentials:    credentialStore,
   106→		fundingSources: fundingSourceStore,
   107→		traces:         traceStore,
   108→		newID:          idGen,
   109→	}
   110→	api.HandlerFromMux(server, mux)
   111→
   112→	// Register non-spec routes (docs, raw spec).
   113→	registerDocsRoutes(mux)
   114→
   115→	// Middleware chain: CORS → request ID → logging → routes.
   116→	var handler http.Handler = mux
   117→	handler = loggingMiddleware(log, handler)
   118→	handler = requestIDMiddleware(handler)
   119→	handler = corsMiddleware(handler)
   120→
   121→	srv := &http.Server{
   122→		Addr:         cfg.addr,
   123→		Handler:      handler,
   124→		ReadTimeout:  10 * time.Second,
   125→		WriteTimeout: 30 * time.Second,
   126→		IdleTimeout:  120 * time.Second,
   127→	}
   128→
   129→	// Graceful shutdown on SIGINT / SIGTERM.
   130→	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   131→	defer stop()
   132→
   133→	go func() {
   134→		log.Info("server listening", "addr", cfg.addr)
   135→		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   136→			log.Error("listen error", "error", err)
   137→		}
   138→	}()
   139→
   140→	<-ctx.Done()
   141→	log.Info("shutting down")
   142→
   143→	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
   144→	defer cancel()
   145→
   146→	return srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   147→}
   148→
   149→// apiServer implements the generated api.ServerInterface.
   150→type apiServer struct {
   151→	log            *slog.Logger
   152→	registry       *connector.Registry
   153→	policyEngine   *policy.RuleEngine
   154→	orchestrator   *approval.InMemoryOrchestrator
   155→	vault          *vault.MemVault
   156→	notifier       notify.Notifier
   157→	intents        *mem.IntentStore
   158→	approvals      *mem.ApprovalStore
   159→	policies       *mem.PolicyStore
   160→	grants         *mem.GrantStore
   161→	executions     *mem.ExecutionStore
   162→	connectors     *mem.ConnectorStore
   163→	credentials    *mem.CredentialStore
   164→	fundingSources *mem.FundingSourceStore
   165→	traces         *mem.TraceStore
   166→	newID          func() string
   167→}
   168→
   169→// config holds runtime configuration sourced from environment variables.
   170→type config struct {
   171→	addr        string
   172→	databaseURL string
   173→}
   174→
   175→func configFromEnv() config {
   176→	addr := os.Getenv("AILERON_ADDR")
   177→	if addr == "" {
   178→		addr = ":8080"
   179→	}
   180→	return config{
   181→		addr:        addr,
   182→		databaseURL: os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL"),
   183→	}
   184→}
   185→

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me provide a comprehensive report on the Aileron project's test patterns.

## Aileron Project Test Patterns Summary

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. **Test Framework & Assertions**

The project uses:
- **Standard Go testing package** (`testing.T` with `t.Fatalf`, `t.Errorf`)
- **No assertion library yet**, but testify (v1.11.1) is available as a transitive dependency via kin-openapi
- The existing tests use manual error checking rather than assertion helpers

### 2. **Integration Test Setup**

**Location:** `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/test/integration/`

**Files:**
- `health_test.go` - Single existing integration test
- `openapi_validator.go` - Shared validation helper
- `go.mod` - Test module with dependencies
- `go.sum` - Locked dependency versions

**Key characteristics:**
- Uses `//go:build integration` build tag to separate integration tests from unit tests
- No unit tests currently exist in `core/` or `sdk/go/` directories
- Tests are run via `task test:integration` with environment variables

### 3. **How Integration Tests Connect to the Server**

The existing `health_test.go` pattern:

```
1. Get API URL from environment or default to http://localhost:8080
2. Make HTTP requests directly using net/http.Client
3. Validate responses against the OpenAPI spec
```

**Environment Configuration:**
- `AILERON_API_URL` - Defaults to `http://localhost:8080`
- `AILERON_UI_URL` - Defaults to `http://localhost:3000` (for UI tests)
- `AILERON_OPENAPI_SPEC` - Path to spec file (auto-discovered if not set)

### 4. **OpenAPI Validation Pattern**

**File:** `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/test/integration/openapi_validator.go`

Key helper function `validateResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response)`:
- Loads OpenAPI spec using `kin-openapi` (v0.133.0)
- Uses gorilla/mux router for route matching
- Validates response against the spec using `openapi3filter.ValidateResponse`
- Prevents hostname/port validation by clearing `doc.Servers`
- Skips full spec validation due to OpenAPI 3.1 compatibility issues
- Uses sync.Once for single-load optimization

**Dependencies:**
- `github.com/getkin/kin-openapi v0.133.0` - OpenAPI parsing and validation
- `github.com/gorilla/mux v1.8.0` - HTTP router used for spec routing
- `github.com/oasdiff/yaml*` - YAML handling utilities

### 5. **Test Execution & CI**

**Taskfile.yml Test Tasks:**

```yaml
test:go          # Unit tests: ./core/... ./sdk/go/... ./cmd/aileron-mcp/...
test:integration # Integration tests with environment variables
test              # Runs all tests
```

**CI Pipeline:**
```
generate → lint → test:go → build → up (docker compose) → test:integration → down
```

**Run command:**
```bash
go test -tags=integration -race ./test/integration/...
```

### 6. **Server Structure for Testing**

**Server Implementation:** `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/server/`

- Uses standard `net/http` (no external web framework)
- HTTP handler mux at core/server/main.go
- Middleware chain: CORS → requestID → logging → routes
- All stores are in-memory during testing
- Graceful shutdown support via signal handling
- JSON request/response encoding with proper error handling

**Available Endpoints:**
- `/v1/health` - Health check (no auth required)
- `/v1/intents` - Create action intent
- `/v1/approvals/*` - Approval management
- `/v1/policies/*` - Policy management
- `/v1/executions/*` - Execution tracking
- And more (see openapi.yaml for full spec)

### 7. **Important Test Files Locations**

- Existing integration test: `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/test/integration/health_test.go`
- OpenAPI validation helper: `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/test/integration/openapi_validator.go`
- Integration test module: `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/test/integration/go.mod`
- OpenAPI spec: `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/api/openapi.yaml`
- Server handlers: `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/server/handlers.go`
- Server main: `/Users/alr/git/ALRubinger/aileron/core/server/main.go`

### 8. **Recommended Patterns for New Tests**

Based on what exists, follow these patterns:

1. **Use build tags:** `//go:build integration` at the top
2. **Get API URL:** Use `os.Getenv("AILERON_API_URL")` with default fallback
3. **Make requests:** Use standard `net/http.Client` directly
4. **Validate responses:** Call `validateResponse(t, resp)` to check against OpenAPI spec
5. **Error handling:** Use `t.Fatalf()` for critical failures, `t.Errorf()` for validation failures
6. **Helper functions:** Mark helpers with `t.Helper()` for clearer error messages
7. **Test organization:** One test file per feature area with build tags

content.content:
1→//go:build integration
     2→
     3→package integration
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"runtime"
    11→	"sync"
    12→	"testing"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/openapi3"
    15→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/openapi3filter"
    16→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/routers"
    17→	"github.com/getkin/kin-openapi/routers/gorillamux"
    18→)
    19→
    20→var (
    21→	specRouter routers.Router
    22→	specOnce   sync.Once
    23→	specErr    error
    24→)
    25→
    26→func loadRouter() (routers.Router, error) {
    27→	specOnce.Do(func() {
    28→		specPath := specFilePath()
    29→		loader := openapi3.NewLoader()
    30→		doc, err := loader.LoadFromFile(specPath)
    31→		if err != nil {
    32→			specErr = err
    33→			return
    34→		}
    35→		// Clear servers so validation doesn't check host/port.
    36→		doc.Servers = nil
    37→		// Skip doc.Validate() — kin-openapi's validator does not fully
    38→		// support OpenAPI 3.1 and rejects valid 3.1 keywords like
    39→		// schema-level "examples". Response validation still works.
    40→		specRouter, specErr = gorillamux.NewRouter(doc)
    41→	})
    42→	return specRouter, specErr
    43→}
    44→
    45→// specFilePath returns the absolute path to the OpenAPI spec.
    46→func specFilePath() string {
    47→	if p := os.Getenv("AILERON_OPENAPI_SPEC"); p != "" {
    48→		return p
    49→	}
    50→	// Default: relative to this source file -> ../../core/api/openapi.yaml
    51→	_, thisFile, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
    52→	return filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(thisFile), "..", "..", "core", "api", "openapi.yaml")
    53→}
    54→
    55→// validateResponse checks that an HTTP response conforms to the OpenAPI spec.
    56→func validateResponse(t *testing.T, resp *http.Response) {
    57→	t.Helper()
    58→
    59→	router, err := loadRouter()
    60→	if err != nil {
    61→		t.Fatalf("failed to load OpenAPI spec: %v", err)
    62→	}
    63→
    64→	route, pathParams, err := router.FindRoute(resp.Request)
    65→	if err != nil {
    66→		t.Fatalf("no matching route in spec for %s %s: %v", resp.Request.Method, resp.Request.URL.Path, err)
    67→	}
    68→
    69→	input := &openapi3filter.ResponseValidationInput{
    70→		RequestValidationInput: &openapi3filter.RequestValidationInput{
    71→			Request:    resp.Request,
    72→			PathParams: pathParams,
    73→			Route:      route,
    74→		},
    75→		Status: resp.StatusCode,
    76→		Header: resp.Header,
    77→		Body:   resp.Body,
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	if err := openapi3filter.ValidateResponse(context.Background(), input); err != nil {
    81→		t.Errorf("response does not match OpenAPI spec: %v", err)
    82→	}
    83→}
    84→
